definition: The broad commodity complex is not experiencing a unified supercycle but is split into three distinct regimes: electrification metals (structurally tight), hydrocarbons (managed via OPEC+ price bands), and agriculture (episodically spiking). This trifurcation invalidates any monolithic supercycle narrative.
evidence: Seven of eight agents independently reject the unified supercycle framing
Supply elasticity ranges 10x across commodities (shale oil ~0.5-1.0 vs. copper ~0.05)
Divergent inventory data and curve structures across sub-complexes
Agriculture has biologically constrained 6-18 month supply response differing from both metals and energy
Survived all four pairwise debates; pair_1 called this the strongest finding
Supply elasticity variance spans 10x across commodities
Agricultural spikes are episodic and mean-reverting within 12-24 months
All 8 agents independently derived this from different analytical lenses
open_questions: Could a synchronized geopolitical shock re-correlate all three complexes temporarily?
At what price levels does substitution across complexes become material?
Does trifurcation break down during correlated macro shocks as demonstrated by March 19, 2026 sell-off?
Does the aggregate +0.28 equity correlation mask bimodal distributions along trifurcation lines?
